summary: Completed comprehensive testing of SkillSync AI Bearer token auth migration. All backend APIs (14/14 tests passed - 100%). Frontend login, registration, logout, and protected routes all working correctly. Fixed 2 issues: (1) MainDashboard.js handleSaveProfile was using withCredentials:true instead of Bearer token headers, (2) ProtectedRoute.js was not properly redirecting unauthenticated users.

issues: Font CORS error for fontshare.com - cosmetic only, doesn't affect functionality

passed_tests: POST /api/auth/login - returns access_token and refresh_token in JSON body (not cookies)
POST /api/auth/register - creates new user and returns tokens in JSON body
GET /api/auth/me - returns user info with Bearer token in Authorization header
Frontend Login: fill email 'admin@skillsync.ai', password 'Admin@123', click Sign In - redirects to dashboard without 'Network Error'
Frontend Register: switch to register mode, create new user - redirects to home/dashboard
Frontend Logout: removes token from localStorage and redirects to home
Landing page loads correctly at / with all 12 sidebar sections visible
ProtectedRoute: accessing /dashboard without login redirects to /login
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: MainDashboard.js handleSaveProfile was using withCredentials:true instead of Bearer token headers - changed to use getAuthHeaders()
Fixed: ProtectedRoute.js was not properly checking authentication state - rewrote to properly verify token and redirect
seed_data_creation: Admin user already exists in database with bcrypt hashed password
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bearer token auth is fully working. Tokens are stored in localStorage (access_token, refresh_token). All API calls use Authorization: Bearer <token> header. No cookies are used for auth. ProtectedRoute properly redirects unauthenticated users to /login.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: CORS conflict - Kubernetes proxy overrides CORS headers with wildcard '*', which conflicts with credentials:'include' for cookies
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Switched from cookie-based auth to Bearer token auth. Tokens returned in JSON body, stored in localStorage, sent via Authorization header
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 14 backend tests pass, frontend login/register/logout flows work without Network Error
